title: Actor Martin Clunes Talks About Playing a Newsreader in a Drama
summary: Martin Clunes, an actor from the show Doc Martin, is getting ready to play Huw Edwards in a new TV drama. Huw Edwards was a famous BBC newsreader who told big news stories for many years. He got into trouble for having bad pictures of children and went to court. Clunes says actors should not judge the people they play. He learned that Edwards was not fun to work with from people who knew him. The drama also shows a young man who was hurt by Edwards. Clunes wants to tell the story of this young man and his family, not just about Edwards. Edwards left the BBC and was sentenced to jail in 2024. Clunes was surprised when he found out it was Edwards in the news.

a person who reads the news on TV or radio
a TV show or play that tells a serious story
a place where judges decide if someone broke the law

What was Huw Edwards' job before he got into trouble?
Why did Huw Edwards go to court?
options: For having bad pictures of children
questions.correct_answer: For having bad pictures of children

questions.question: What does Martin Clunes say actors should not do?
Who else is the drama about besides Huw Edwards?
options: A young man and his family
questions.correct_answer: A young man and his family

questions.question: When was Huw Edwards sentenced to jail?

background_read: Huw Edwards was a well-known newsreader on the BBC for over 40 years, meaning many people in the UK watched him on TV. In 2023, a newspaper reported that a top BBC star was in trouble for paying a teenager for bad pictures. Later, it was revealed to be Huw Edwards. He resigned from the BBC and was charged by the police. In 2024, he pleaded guilty in court and got a suspended jail sentence, which means he doesn't go to jail if he behaves for two years. Martin Clunes is an actor famous for playing Doc Martin, and he is now in a drama about this story.
Article_Structure: WHO: Martin Clunes, an actor, and Huw Edwards, a former BBC newsreader.
WHAT: Clunes is playing Edwards in a drama about Edwards' crimes and the young victim.
WHERE: The story involves the BBC in the UK and a court in Westminster.
WHY: To show the challenges of acting as a real person and to tell the victim's story.

perspectives.description: Martin Clunes believes actors should not judge their characters and wants to focus on the victim's story.
